proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90, plausibly age-like) is associated with pfs_months as a main effect.
Higher levels of the 3-level categorical feature_001 are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Patients with feature_014=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_014=0.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import pearsonr; pearsonr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 shows a strong POSITIVE Pearson correlation with pfs_months (r=0.766, p<1e-300). Each unit of feature_015 corresponds to ~0.176 months PFS in unadjusted regression.

analyses.code: import scipy.stats as st; st.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_001']==v,'pfs_months'] for v in [0,1,2]])
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_001 level: 0=5.27 (n=17592), 1=4.05 (n=24971), 2=2.91 (n=7437). One-way ANOVA p<1e-300. Each step up the ordinal scale lowers PFS by ~1.18 months.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_014==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_014==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_014=1 mean PFS=3.70 vs feature_014=0 mean=5.06; t-test p<1e-300. feature_014=1 patients have ~1.35 months shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 is a binary treatment indicator: patients with feature_027=1 have LONGER pfs_months than those with feature_027=0.
feature_023=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_023=0 (negative prognostic factor).
feature_006=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_006=0 (negative prognostic factor).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_027==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_027==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_027=1 mean PFS=5.09 (n=10022) vs feature_027=0 mean=4.12 (n=39978); t-test p<1e-300. feature_027 is the only binary feature with a POSITIVE main effect on PFS, consistent with it being a treatment indicator. Treatment rate ~20%.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_023==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_023==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_023=1 mean PFS=4.12 (n=20898) vs feature_023=0 mean=4.45 (n=29102); t-test p=8.5e-56.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_006==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_006==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=1 mean PFS=4.11 (n=17657) vs feature_006=0 mean=4.42 (n=32343); t-test p=5.2e-46.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutually adjusting for all features in a multivariable OLS model, feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_019, feature_025, and feature_027 retain large statistically significant effects on pfs_months with the directions seen univariately.
Higher feature_019 (continuous, 1.5-5.5, possibly albumin-like) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher feature_025 (continuous, 0-25) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; X = sm.add_constant(df[features]); m = sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.86. Significant adjusted effects (beta, p): feature_015 +0.176 (p<1e-300); feature_001 -1.171 (p<1e-300); feature_014 -1.362 (p<1e-300); feature_006 -0.287 (p<1e-300); feature_023 -0.354 (p<1e-300); feature_022 -0.357 (p<1e-78); feature_005 -0.005 (p=3.1e-97); feature_019 +0.469 (p<1e-300); feature_021 -0.0004 (p=8e-19); feature_025 -0.076 (p<1e-300); feature_027 +0.943 (p<1e-300). All directions match univariate analysis. feature_027 retains the largest binary positive effect, supporting its identification as the treatment.

analyses.code: pearsonr(df['feature_019'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=0.102 (p=3e-115). Adjusted beta in MV model = +0.469 per unit (p<1e-300). Direction confirmed: higher feature_019 -> longer PFS.

analyses.code: pearsonr(df['feature_025'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=-0.123 (p=2e-167). Adjusted beta = -0.076 per unit (p<1e-300). Direction confirmed: higher feature_025 -> shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 (treatment) is balanced across all candidate prognostic features, suggesting either randomization or no strong selection bias; treatment effect estimates need no propensity adjustment.

analyses.code: for f in features: corr or chi2 of f with feature_027
analyses.result_summary: Treatment rate ~20% in every binary stratum (feature_006 0/1: 0.202/0.198; feature_023 0/1: 0.200/0.201; feature_022 0/1: 0.200/0.206; feature_001 0/1/2: 0.205/0.198/0.197; feature_014 0/1: 0.198/0.202). Pearson r of feature_015, feature_005, feature_019 with feature_027 all |r|<0.01 (all p>0.15). Treatment assignment is essentially random with respect to baseline covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment (feature_027) effect on pfs_months is modified by feature_023: among feature_023=0 patients the treatment effect is positive and larger than +0.5 months, but among feature_023=1 patients the treatment effect is null (<=0.1 months).

Treatment (feature_027) effect on pfs_months is modified by feature_006: among feature_006=0 patients the treatment effect is positive and larger than +0.5 months, but among feature_006=1 patients the treatment effect is null.

Treatment (feature_027) effect on pfs_months is modified by feature_022: among feature_022=0 the treatment effect is positive, but among feature_022=1 the treatment effect is null or slightly negative.

analyses.code: Stratify treatment effect by feature_023; t-test in each stratum.
analyses.result_summary: feature_023=0 (n=29102): tx_eff = +1.656 (m_tx=5.79, m_ctrl=4.13), p<1e-300. feature_023=1 (n=20898): tx_eff = +0.027, p=0.45. Massive effect modification.

analyses.code: Stratify treatment effect by feature_006.
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=0 (n=32343): tx_eff = +1.480, p<1e-300. feature_006=1 (n=17657): tx_eff = +0.026, p=0.52. feature_006=1 abolishes the treatment effect.

analyses.code: Stratify treatment effect by feature_022.
analyses.result_summary: feature_022=0 (n=47728): tx_eff = +1.027, p<1e-300. feature_022=1 (n=2272): tx_eff = -0.156, p=0.15. feature_022=1 abolishes (and slightly reverses) the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint subgroup feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 has a treatment effect substantially larger than +1.5 months, while any combination involving feature_006=1 or feature_023=1 has a near-null treatment effect.

In a regression of pfs_months on tx, feature_006, feature_023, tx*feature_006, tx*feature_023, both interaction terms are statistically significant and negative (each removes a large amount of treatment benefit).

analyses.code: Cross-tabulate tx_eff by (feature_006, feature_023).
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=0,023=0 (n=18853): tx_eff=+2.520 (p~0). feature_006=0,023=1 (n=13490): tx_eff=+0.061 (p=0.17). feature_006=1,023=0 (n=10249): tx_eff=+0.071 (p=0.17). feature_006=1,023=1 (n=7408): tx_eff=-0.040 (p=0.52). The benefit is essentially confined to the doubly-negative cell.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ tx + f006 + f023 + tx:f006 + tx:f023.
analyses.result_summary: tx beta=+2.175 (p<1e-300); tx*feature_006 = -1.471 (p=4e-178); tx*feature_023 = -1.643 (p=2e-235). Both interactions negative and highly significant. Each unfavorable feature subtracts ~1.5 months of treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple-negative subgroup feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 has a still larger treatment effect (>+2.5 months); within the triple-negative subgroup, feature_022=1 specifically eliminates and slightly reverses the treatment effect.

analyses.code: Stratify (feature_006=0, feature_023=0) by feature_022.
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=0 & feature_023=0 & feature_022=0 (n=17372): tx_eff=+2.758 (p~0). feature_006=0 & feature_023=0 & feature_022=1 (n=1481): tx_eff=-0.204 (p=0.13). feature_022 functions as a third effect-modifying biomarker that fully suppresses treatment benefit even in the otherwise responsive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-negative biomarker subgroup, at least one continuous feature has a statistically significant interaction (tx x feature) modifying the treatment effect.
Within the triple-negative biomarker subgroup, feature_005 has a NEGATIVE tx*feature_005 interaction: as feature_005 increases the treatment effect shrinks toward zero.

analyses.code: For each continuous f: OLS pfs ~ tx + f_centered + tx:f_centered within the triple-negative subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Only feature_005 produced a strong interaction within the triple-negative subgroup: tx*feature_005 beta = -0.0649, p=7.4e-136. All other continuous features had |interaction p|>0.02 (most p>0.1). Direction of feature_005 interaction is negative as predicted (higher feature_005 -> smaller treatment effect).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-negative subgroup the treatment effect is sharply thresholded at feature_005 ~5: feature_005 <= 5 yields tx_eff ~+5 months, feature_005 > 5 yields tx_eff ~0.

analyses.code: Sweep threshold t in [2,8] over feature_005 within the triple-negative subgroup; report tx_eff <=t and >t.
analyses.result_summary: Within the triple-negative subgroup: at t=5.0 tx_eff(<=t)=+5.014 (n=9338) and tx_eff(>t)=-0.002 (n=8034). Effect plateau is flat across t in [2,4.75] (~+5.0) and crashes to ~0 above 5. Decile analysis: deciles 1-5 of feature_005 have tx_eff +4.9 to +5.1; decile 6 (4.47-6.02) has +1.81; deciles 7-10 are <0.05. The threshold at feature_005=5 is sharp and corresponds to a natural round value.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full responder subgroup is feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<=5: within this subgroup feature_027 (treatment) increases pfs_months by ~+5 months; outside this subgroup the treatment effect is statistically null and approximately zero.

analyses.code: Define responder = (f006==0)&(f023==0)&(f022==0)&(f005<=5); fit OLS pfs ~ tx + responder + tx:responder.
analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup n=9338 (treated=1905, ctrl=7433): mean PFS treated=9.103 vs ctrl=4.089 (diff +5.014, p~0). Non-responder n=40662: mean PFS treated=4.147 vs ctrl=4.123 (diff +0.024, p=0.36). Interaction term tx*responder = +4.99 (p<1e-300). Outside the subgroup the treatment provides no detectable benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the four subgroup conditions (feature_006=0, feature_023=0, feature_022=0, feature_005<=5) is necessary: removing any one dilutes the treatment effect because the dropped condition admits non-responders.

analyses.code: Leave-one-out drop each predicate from the responder definition and report tx_eff.
analyses.result_summary: Drop feature_005<=5 (keep f006=0 & f023=0 & f022=0, n=17372): tx_eff = +2.758. Drop feature_006=0 (n=14492): tx_eff = +3.303. Drop feature_023=0 (n=16612): tx_eff = +2.850. Drop feature_022=0 (n=10117): tx_eff = +4.624. The full 4-predicate definition (n=9338, +5.014) outperforms every leave-one-out version, confirming each predicate is necessary.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An unsupervised data-driven tree-based subgroup discovery (virtual twins: train RF on treated and control, predict counterfactuals, fit a regression tree on the predicted treatment effect) will independently rediscover the same four-feature responder rule.

analyses.code: rf_t.fit(treated); rf_c.fit(control); tau = rf_t.predict(X) - rf_c.predict(X); DecisionTreeRegressor(max_depth=4).fit(X, tau)
analyses.result_summary: Top-of-tree splits in order: feature_005<=4.95 -> feature_023<=0.5 -> feature_006<=0.5 -> feature_022<=0.5 -> predicted tx effect = +4.89 (the only leaf with large positive predicted effect). Every other leaf has predicted tx effect within [-0.18, +0.30]. The data-driven tree exactly reproduces the manually-derived responder rule.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect inside the responder subgroup remains ~+5 months after multivariable adjustment for prognostic covariates (feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_019, feature_025, feature_021, feature_031), confirming the effect is not explained by within-subgroup confounding.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ tx + f015 + f001 + f014 + f019 + f025 + f021 + f031 within responder subgroup (n=9338).
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment effect = +4.980 (SE 0.004, p<1e-300). Adjusted prognostic effects (feature_015 +0.177, feature_001 -1.177, feature_014 -1.378, feature_019 +0.487, feature_025 -0.079, feature_021 -0.0005) match the full-cohort multivariable model. Treatment benefit is robust to adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After conditioning on the four-feature responder rule, no additional binary or continuous feature meaningfully modifies the treatment effect (every additional split produces a tx effect within +/-0.3 months of the subgroup average).

analyses.code: Within the responder subgroup, stratify tx_eff by every other binary feature and by quartiles of every other continuous feature.
analyses.result_summary: Within the responder subgroup, all binary features yield tx_eff in [+4.9, +5.2] in both levels. feature_011=0/1: +5.00/+5.24 (rare 5% subset). Quartiles of feature_019 give +4.94/+5.04/+4.96/+5.12. Across feature_001 (0/1/2): +5.0/+4.97/+5.06 (within the resp subgroup). No additional effect modifier was found.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among non-responder patients (any of feature_006=1, feature_023=1, feature_022=1, or feature_005>5), there is no patient profile that recovers a meaningful treatment effect (>+0.5 months).

analyses.code: Stratify treatment effect within the 11 non-responder cells of (f006, f023, f022) x (f005<=5, f005>5).
analyses.result_summary: Across all eleven non-responder cells (each with n>=200) the treatment effect ranges from -0.235 to +0.113 with no statistically significant cell. The largest non-responder cell tx effect is +0.113 in (f006=1,f023=0,f022=0,f005<=5; n=5154), p=0.12. No treatment-responsive subpopulation exists outside the four-feature rule.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011=1 (a rare ~3% binary marker) is associated with longer pfs_months as a main effect.
feature_022=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months as a main effect, independent of its role as a treatment-effect modifier.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on feature_011.
analyses.result_summary: feature_011=1 mean PFS=4.526 (n=1506) vs 0 mean=4.305 (n=48494); univariate t-test p=2.3e-4. In multivariable model the adjusted effect shrinks to +0.020 (p=0.37), so the univariate effect is largely explained by other covariates.

analyses.code: MV OLS coefficient for feature_022.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_022 main effect = -0.357 (SE 0.019, p<1e-78). Independent prognostic effect (~0.36 month decrement) on top of its tx-modifying role.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 is one of the strongest prognostic factors after adjustment: each increase of one level lowers pfs_months by approximately 1.17 months.
feature_015 retains a strong positive association with pfs_months in the multivariable model (beta ~+0.176 per unit), making it the dominant continuous prognostic factor.

analyses.code: MV OLS coefficient for feature_001.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted beta for feature_001 = -1.171 per level (SE 0.006, p<1e-300). Each level corresponds to ~1.17 months shorter PFS (matching univariate stepwise pattern 5.27 -> 4.05 -> 2.91).

analyses.code: MV OLS coefficient for feature_015.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted beta for feature_015 = +0.176 per unit (SE 0.0004, p<1e-300). Direction is positive (counterintuitive if feature_015 is age, but consistent univariately too); over its 30-90 range it accounts for ~10 months of variation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the full cohort, no continuous feature other than feature_005 has a meaningful treatment-effect interaction with feature_027.

analyses.code: For each continuous f: OLS pfs ~ tx + f_centered + tx:f_centered (full cohort).
analyses.result_summary: Only feature_005 had p<1e-50 for tx*f interaction (beta=-0.0253, p=8.7e-58). Other continuous features all had |interaction p|>0.10 (e.g., feature_019 p=0.12, feature_007 p=0.11, feature_013 p=0.13). feature_005 is the unique continuous treatment-effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005<=5 alone (without the three binary biomarker conditions) does NOT produce a clean treatment-responsive subgroup; the binary biomarkers are independently necessary.

analyses.code: Among feature_005<=5 (whole cohort): stratify tx_eff by (f006, f023, f022).
analyses.result_summary: feature_005<=5 cohort n=26997: overall tx_eff = +1.787 (diluted). Within feature_005<=5, the tx effect is again concentrated in (f006=0,f023=0,f022=0) cell: +5.014 (n=9338). Cells (1,*,*), (*,1,*), (*,*,1) within feature_005<=5 all have tx_eff in [-0.22, +0.11]. Confirms feature_005<=5 is not sufficient on its own.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final main-effect ranking on pfs_months in the multivariable OLS: feature_015 (positive, dominant continuous), feature_001 (large negative ordinal), feature_014 (large negative binary), feature_027 (large positive binary, treatment), feature_019 (positive continuous), feature_023 (negative binary), feature_022 (negative binary), feature_006 (negative binary), feature_025 (negative continuous), feature_005 (small negative continuous).

analyses.code: Re-read MV OLS coefficients.
analyses.result_summary: Top 10 absolute t-statistics in MV OLS: feature_015 (+0.176, t=453.6); feature_001 (-1.171, t=-205.7); feature_014 (-1.362, t=-175.6); feature_027 (+0.943, t=97.9); feature_025 (-0.076, t=-76.1); feature_019 (+0.469, t=61.0); feature_023 (-0.354, t=-43.9); feature_006 (-0.287, t=-35.5); feature_005 (-0.005, t=-21.0); feature_022 (-0.357, t=-18.9). All match predicted directions; treatment is the strongest binary positive, feature_014 the strongest binary negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: The treatment feature_027 prolongs pfs_months by approximately +5 months specifically and only in patients satisfying ALL of: (1) feature_006=0, (2) feature_023=0, (3) feature_022=0, (4) feature_005<=5. Outside this 4-feature subgroup the treatment effect is approximately 0 months. Each of the four predicates is necessary: dropping any one shrinks the in-subgroup treatment effect by 0.4-2.3 months because the dropped predicate readmits non-responders.

analyses.code: Final confirmatory test: OLS pfs ~ tx + responder + tx:responder where responder = (f006==0)&(f023==0)&(f022==0)&(f005<=5).
analyses.result_summary: Final responder subgroup n=9338 (18.7% of cohort, of whom 1905 received treatment). Mean PFS in responders: treated 9.10, control 4.09 -> tx_eff = +5.01 (p<1e-300). Mean PFS in non-responders (n=40662): treated 4.15, control 4.12 -> tx_eff = +0.024 (p=0.36, NS). Interaction p<1e-300. The treatment provides no detectable benefit outside the 4-feature subgroup.
